entity.name: `<svg>` (Inline SVG)
entity.description: Embed SVG vector graphics directly in markup
entity.descriptionHtml: Embed SVG vector graphics directly in markup
entity.descriptionClean: Embed SVG vector graphics directly in markup

example.codeHighlighted: <svg> <circle cx="50" cy="50" r="50" /> </svg>

webFeature.description_html: The SVG image format, represented by the <svg> element, creates two-dimensional vector graphics with declarative or scripted interaction and animation.
webFeature.name: SVG

entity.name: Browser issues
entity.description: Browser support issues, implementation bugs, cross‑browser implementation differences or inconsistencies, browser prioritization, speed of adoption of new features, any issues where specific browsers, browser engines, or browser companies are mentioned (and it’s not to praise them).

entity.name: Non-rectangular element shape
entity.description: Giving an element a genuinely non-rectangular shape that the box itself takes on — so its border, box-shadow, and outline follow the new outline — rather than faking it by clipping the element (which drops borders/shadows) or layering shapes via pseudo-elements.
entity.descriptionHtml: Giving an element a genuinely non-rectangular shape that the box itself takes on — so its border, box-shadow, and outline follow the new outline — rather than faking it by clipping the element (which drops borders/shadows) or layering shapes via pseudo-elements.
entity.descriptionClean: Giving an element a genuinely non-rectangular shape that the box itself takes on — so its border, box-shadow, and outline follow the new outline — rather than faking it by clipping the element (which drops borders/shadows) or layering shapes via pseudo-elements.

entity.name: Developer Experience
entity.description: Issues related not to being unable to achieve something, but to *how* it's achieved. Bad syntax, confusing APIs, performance issues, etc.
entity.descriptionHtml: Issues related not to being unable to achieve something, but to how it's achieved. Bad syntax, confusing APIs, performance issues, etc.
entity.descriptionClean: Issues related not to being unable to achieve something, but to how it's achieved. Bad syntax, confusing APIs, performance issues, etc.

webFeature.description_html: The clip-path CSS property and SVG attribute set the visible area of an element. Everything outside the area will be hidden.
webFeature.name: clip-path

example.codeHighlighted: p { display: -webkit-box; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; -webkit-box-orient: vertical; overflow: hidden; }

webFeature.description_html: The line-clamp CSS property limits the text in a block container to a certain number of lines. The prefixed -webkit-line-clamp is widely supported but only works with -webkit-box-orient: vertical in combination with display: -webkit-box or display: -webkit-inline-box.
webFeature.name: line-clamp

entity.descriptionClean: General issues related to the browser's process of painting pixels on the screen, including layout, style recalculation, and compositing.

webFeature.description_html: The linear-gradient() and radial-gradient() CSS functions and their repeating counterparts create backgrounds that progress smoothly between multiple colors.
webFeature.name: Gradients

example.codeHighlighted: .clipped { clip-path: shape( from top left, hline to 100%, vline to 100%, curve to 0% 100% with 50% 0%, ); }

webFeature.description_html: The shape() CSS function creates shapes with a series of commands like line, move, and curve. It can be used with clip-path and shape-outside.
webFeature.name: shape()

resources.title: Leading-Trim: The Future of Digital Typesetting

webFeature.description_html: The text-box CSS property sets the spacing above and below text based on a font's typographic features. For example, text-box: trim-both ex alphabetic trims the top to the top of the letter x and the bottom to the bottom of most letters, without descenders.
webFeature.name: text-box
